channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #824 Hints and Answers for Today, June 5, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #824 for Friday, June 5, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 5, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #824 are all terms related to fighting.

General Strands Hints for June 5

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: The Super Smash Bros. game on Nintendo GameCube.
  • Hint 2: The Super Smash Bros. game on Nintendo Wii.
  • Hint 3: WWE’s most recent Premium Live Event was ___ in Italy.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #824 for June 5, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 5, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FIGHT
  • SALE
  • RANG
  • SEED
  • REEL

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #824 On June 5, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SCUFFLE
  • MELEE
  • BRAWL
  • SCRAP
  • CLASH
  • BRAWL
  • SKIRMISH
  • SPANAGRAM: FIGHTINGWORDS

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #824?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 6 will be Strands Game #825.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-824-hints-and-answers-for-today-june-5-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Paramount Unveils New Game Studio; First Reveal Coming Today
item.content:encoded:
Paramount Skydance has just revealed a brand-new division, called Paramount Games Studio, and players will see its first reveal today.

A report by Variety confirms the creation of Paramount Games Studio, an amalgamation of its existing game studios, Skydance Interactive, and Skydance New Media. The report also confirms that the announcement of their first AAA title will come later tonight at Summer Game Fest 2026. Players have been informed that the development of Skydance New Media’s untitled Star Wars game and Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra remains unchanged after the formation of the new studio.

Tony Discroll has been appointed as the president of Paramount Games Studio. He will be joined by Skydance veterans, including Dan Prigg, who will serve as executive vice president and head of games for Paramount, and Shawn Kittelsen, who will serve as senior vice president, head of creative & production. Additionally, there have been changes in the leadership of Skydance New Media: one co-president, Amy Hennig, will remain with the studio as creative director, while the other co-president, Julian Beak, has left the company.

Here’s Tony Discroll’s statement on the formation of Paramount Games Studio:

“This division launch marks a meaningful evolution in how we think about games – not as an extension of our business, but as a core pillar of our content strategy alongside film, television, and streaming.

We are committed to creating exceptional games for every type of player, from casual to AAA, and building enduring experiences across our beloved Paramount IP and original worlds that deepen fan engagement and drive long-term growth.”

In other news, Paramount+ and HBO Max will merge into a single service if the Skydance acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is approved. What are your thoughts on the formation of Paramount Games Studio? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about No Rest for the Wicked’s XBOX release being delayed by the Series S. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/paramount-unveils-new-game-studio-first-reveal-coming-today/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Rest for the Wicked XBOX Release Delayed Due to Series S
item.content:encoded:
The creative director of No Rest for the Wicked has clarified why the game is not releasing on XBOX and Switch 2 on the same date as PS5 and PC.

Just a couple of days ago, developers announced that No Rest for the Wicked 1.0 is set to launch in October 2026. It will feature 100 hours of content, a new class system, and the final story arc. However, players were left confused because the developers provided a release window only for PS5 and PC, without mentioning other platforms.

Series S and Switch 2 Optimization Issues Have Delayed No Rest for the Wicked’s Release on the Platforms

In a new post on X (formerly Twitter), the creative director of No Rest for the Wicked, Thomas Malher, revealed that the Series S was the reason for the XBOX release delay. Malher first spoke about how the developers spend a lot of time analyzing “what it takes to ship Wicked at the quality level we expect on each platform.” After all this time, they are confident that the expected experience can be delivered on PC, Steam Deck, and PS5 for the October launch.

The issue with the XBOX and Nintendo releases stems from optimization challenges with the Series S and the Switch 2. Malher notes that these platforms require “more hardcore optimizations” because of their “smaller memory budget.” The game No Rest of the Wicked constantly simulates and streams a living, breathing world in the background, which requires loading and unloading large amounts of data while playing.

They do not want to rush the port and deliver a subpar version. Their goal is to make Wicked “feel amazing” across all platforms. Malher’s concluding statement confirms that No Rest for the Wicked will eventually be released on the XBOX Series X/S and Switch 2, but they want to ensure it’s the best version when it arrives.

Check out Mahler’s full statement below:

Folks,

Obviously some people are disappointed that we don’t ship No Rest for the Wicked in October day and date on Xbox and Switch 2 as well.

Since the rumor mill is already going wild on us having accepted some exclusivity deal or something… Folks, chill, none of that is the case.

We’ve spent months assessing what it takes to ship Wicked at the quality level we expect on each platform. PC, Steam Deck and PS5 are where we’re confident we can deliver the experience we want for our October launch.

On Xbox Series S and Switch 2, more hardcore optimizations are required mainly due to the smaller memory budget there. Wicked is constantly simulating and streaming a living, breathing world in the background, loading and unloading huge amounts of data as you play. Every megabyte matters.

We’re not in the business of shipping shoddy ports. We were always insanely proud of being able to ship BOTH Ori games on Switch at 60fps, no compromises. But that took time – and the same thing is true here.

Our goal is simple: Wherever you play Wicked, it should feel amazing. We know what’s required to get there, and we’re continuing that work.

No Rest for the Wicked is coming to all current platforms. We just want to make sure that when it arrives, it’s the best version it can possibly be. *thumbs up*

In other news, No Rest for the Wicked has crossed 1.5 million units sold, within 15 days of the previous milestone in February 2026. Additionally, Moon Studios plans to raise No Rest for the Wicked’s price at full release. What are your thoughts on No Rest for the Wicked’s XBOX release being delayed due to Series S optimization issues? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the release date and trailer for Hot Wheels Infinite Rush. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/no-rest-for-the-wicked-xbox-release-delayed-due-to-series-s/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Escape from Tarkov: Arena Bug Inspired New CheckPoint: Knives Out Mode
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has today kicked off an all-new temporary mode in Escape from Tarkov: Arena, dubbed ‘CheckPoint: Knives Out’, which was inspired by a recent technical issue. It’s not often that a bug directly contributes to a streak of innovation in a game, but every cloud has a silver lining, and this one has introduced knife-only battles to Arena.

BSG confirmed that the special mode will start today and will run until June 9th, allowing players to participate in knife fights on Fort and Bay 5 to secure boosted XP multipliers and a unique achievement. Like other similar events, when it’s gone, it’s gone.

CheckPoint: Knives Out Starts in Tarkov: Arena

Battlestate wrote in a recent press release that hte idea for the CheckPoint: Knives Out mode came from a bug that surfaced with the 0.5.1.1 patch. This error temporarily restricted players to melee-only damage, rendering conventional, ballistic weaponry entirely useless.

Bizarrely, players leapt on the topic and stressed their enjoyment, and lo and behold, Battlestate decided to make it a more fleshed-out event with a new achievement.

Here’s how it works:

  • Combat Rules: Complete restriction on firearms. All participants enter the “Fort” and “Bay 5” arenas equipped exclusively with melee weapons.
  • Progress Boost: XP earned in the CheckPoint mode is doubled (2x), and Battle Pass XP is increased by 1.5 (1.5x), respectively.
  • Quests and Rewards: A special chain of 3 quests has been added to the game. Completing the entire chain awards a unique event achievement.

In the Escape from Tarkov world, melee weapons are often easily overlooked, unless you’re talking about a more practical item like a Red Rebel pick in the base extraction shooter game. This mode is a great reminder that they a) do exist, and b) can be the source of stacks of entertainment.

Remember: Knives Out will only run until June 8th, so participate while you can.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re enjoying Escape from Tarkov: Arena.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a new Hot Wheels game has had its release date revealed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/escape-from-tarkov-arena-checkpoint-knives-out/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rainbow Six Siege Stage 1 Schedules—Esports World Cup Qualifiers, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has announced that the Rainbow Six Siege Stage 1 circuit starts very soon, and that it’s the opener of the qualification path to the fast-approaching Esports World Cup. Following a drastic change, the Esports World Cup will now take place in Paris instead of Riyadh, which means millions more fans are more likely to attend, eager to see all the action, including Rainbow Six esports.

From the Asia Pacific League to the CNL, you’ll find every Rainbow Six Stage 1 schedule below, including how teams qualify for the Esports World Cup and what the prize pools are worth.

Quick Navigation

  • North America League
  • South America League
  • Europe MENA League
  • China League
  • How Does Stage 1 Set Up for the Esports World Cup?
  • How to Watch Rainbow Six Siege Esports

Be warned – this is a long one! But every schedule, team list, and prize pool is outlined below for Stage 1 of the Rainbow Six Siege esports circuit.

Asia Pacific League

Everything in the opening stage of the Rainbow Six Siege esports schedule towards the EWC starts with the APAC League. Here’s the full Oceania roster for this season:

  • Enterprise
  • Shaiikademy
  • Chiefs EC
  • Team Anios
  • Man Esports LFO
  • Barbie Boys
  • Rock LFO
  • 6Targets

Here’s the R6 APAC League Stage schedule for the APL Oceania:

Round 1: June 8th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Man LFO
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Chiefs ESC

Round 2: June 9th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Chiefs ESC
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs 6Targets

Round 3: June 15th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Team Anios
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs Chiefs ESC

Round 4: June 16th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Man LFO
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs 6Targets
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – ENTERPRISE vs Rock LFO

Round 5: June 22nd

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs 6Targets
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Rock LFO
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs ENTERPRISE

Round 6: June 23rd

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs Team Anios
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs Man LFO
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – ENTREPRISE vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs Rock LFO

Round 7: June 29th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs 6Targets
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Team Anios
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Chiefs ESC
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs ENTERPRISE

And here’s the roster breakdown for the APL Asia:

  • Elevate
  • Soul’s Heart Esport
  • Weibo Gaming
  • Team Orchid
  • Daystar
  • Unreal Nightmare
  • Fury
  • HL Tauri

If you’re interested in watching the APL Asia, here’s the Stage 1 schedule:

Round 1: June 10th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs HL Tauri Esport
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs FURY Global
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs Weibo Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Elevate

Round 2: June 11th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Team Orchid
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Elevate vs Weibo Gaming

Round 3: June 17th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs HL Tauri Esport
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Weibo Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs Elevate
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs DAYSTAR

Round 4: June 18th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs FURY Global
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Weibo Gaming vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Elevate vs Team Orchid

Round 5: June 24th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs DAYSTAR
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs Elevate
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Weibo Gaming

Round 6: June 25th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs DAYSTAR
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Elevate vs FURY Global
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Weibo Gaming vs HL Tauri Esport

Round 7: June 30th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Team Orchid
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Elevate
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Weibo Gaming

And finally, the APL North roster:

  • SCARZ
  • DPLUS KIA
  • CAG Osaka
  • Kinotrope GAming
  • BNK FearX
  • LFO No Name
  • Trippy Main Telecom
  • Can You Be My Enemy?

Here’s their schedule for Stage 1:

Round 1: June 12th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs LFO No Name
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs Kinotrope Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs CAG Osaka

Round 2: June 13th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs SCARZ
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – CAG OSAKA vs LFO No Name
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs Trippy Main Telecom

Round 3: June 19th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs LFO No Name
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs DPlus Kia
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs CAG Osaka

Round 4: June 20th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – LFO No Name vs SCARZ
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – CAG Osaka vs Trippy Main Telecom
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs Kinotrope Gaming

Round 5: June 26th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs DPlus Kia
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs BNK FearX
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs LFO No Name
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – SCARZ vs CAG Osaka

Round 6: June 27th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs CAG Osaka
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs Trippy Main Telecom
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs LFO No Name

Round 7: July 1st

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – LFO No Name vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs SCARZ
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – CAG Osaka vs Kinotrope Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs DPlus Kia

APL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $15,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $11,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $8,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $7,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $5,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $4,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $2,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $2,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $0 / 35 SI points

North America League

Here’s the spread of teams competing in the NAL Stage 1 in the Rainbow Six Siege space:

  • Cloud9
  • Shopify Rebellion
  • M80
  • 100 Thieves
  • Spacestation
  • Five Fears
  • Wildcard
  • 1 of 30
  • DARKZERO
  • Outlast

And the round robin schedule for the first stage:

Round 1: June 10th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs 1 of 30
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs Spacestation Gaming
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Wildcard vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs Cloud9
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Outlast

Round 2: June 11th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs 1 of 30
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Outlast
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Cloud9

Round 3: June 12th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs 1 of 30
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Five Fears
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Outlast
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Shopify Rebellion

Round 4: June 18th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Five Fears
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Outlast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – M80 vs Cloud9
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Wildcard

Round 5: June 19th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs 1 of 30
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs Outlast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs Five Fears
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Wildcard

Round 6: June 25th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Outlast vs Cloud9
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs Five Fears
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – M80 vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Wildcard
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs DarkZero Esports

Round 7: June 26th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs Five Fears
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Outcast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs Spacestation Gaming
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs 1 of 30

Round 8: July 2nd

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Wildcard
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs SpaceStation Gaming
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Outlast vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs 1 of 30

Round 9: July 3rd

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs Wildcard
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Cloud9
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs SpaceStation Gaming
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Five Fears

NAL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $25,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $22,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $19,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $16,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $13,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $11,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $10,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $8,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $8,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : $8,000.00 / 0 SI points

South America League

Here are the teams competing in the South America League in Stage 1:

  • FURIA
  • Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Fluxo W7M
  • Black Dragons
  • FaZe Clan
  • LOS
  • Team Liquid Alienware
  • Imperial Esports
  • LOUD
  • INTZ

And here’s the full list of fixtures in the Stage 1 schedule for the Rainbow Six Siege esports space:

Round 1: June 13th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – INTZ vs TL Alienware
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs FaZe Clan
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Black Dragons vs Fluxo.W7M
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – LOUD vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – LOS vs Furia

Round 2: June 14th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs TL Alienware
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – INTZ vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Black Dragons vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – LOS vs LOUD
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs Fluxo W7M

Round 3: June 20th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Fluxo W7M
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – TL Alienware vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – INTZ vs LOUD
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – Black Dragons vs LOS
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs Ninjas in Pyjamas

Round 4: June 21st

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – TL Alienware vs LOUD
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs LOS
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – INTZ vs Black Dragons

Round 5: June 27th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Furia vs LOUD
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs LOS
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Ninjas in Pyjamas

  • 5th : CN¥ 90,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : CN¥ 85,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : CN¥ 80,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : CN¥ 75,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : CN¥ 70,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : CN¥ 65,000.00 / 0 SI points

How Does Stage 1 Set Up for the Esports World Cup?

This is a super simple concept. At the end of Stage 1, every region will undergo a finals round, pushing through a double-elimination bracket to make it to the grand final. Either the winner or the finalists for each region will qualify for the Esports World Cup, slated to land in Paris from July onwards.

Here’s how it works for each region:

APL Oceania

The two participating teams at the APL Oceania Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

APL Asia

The winner of the APL Asia Stage 1 Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

APL North

The winner of the APL North Stage 1 Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

NAL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

SAL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

EML

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris. Team Secret is directly invited to EWC thanks to their win last year.

CNL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

How to Watch Rainbow Six Siege Esports

If you want to tune into the Rainbow Six Stage 1 action as it unfolds, make sure to follow the R6 Esports Twitch channel.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re watching this year’s Esports World Cup.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that League’s Bin has been fined heavily by Riot Games

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
